justifications.clarity: Prose is consistently lucid, well-paced, and authoritative throughout a very long chapter (~2,720 lines). Financial motivations are woven into every conceptual step. Notation is defined before use and remains consistent across all sections. Section transitions are explicitly motivated with forward/backward pointers. Context-setting vignettes open practitioner sections effectively. The formerly dangling \ref{sec:limitations-hallucinations} is now correctly resolved to \ref{sec:hallucinations}. Cross-chapter references (def:word-embedding, sec:classical-tfidf, sec:embeddings) are intentional intra-book references to ch01 that resolve correctly in compilation. No broken or ambiguous references remain.
justifications.rigor: All key propositions are formally stated and proved: vanishing-gradient bound (Proposition 3.2), attention weights forming a probability distribution, sqrt(d_k) variance normalisation, sinusoidal PE relative-position linearity. SBERT mean-pool denominator correctly divides by sum(m_j) (not n). LSTM constant-error carousel explanation is appropriately qualified with the caveat that forget-gate saturation can still cause decay. Forward-versus-reverse KL remark in distillation is technically precise. Marginalisation clarification note in Definition for reasoning models correctly distinguishes exact marginalisation from the single-sample Monte Carlo approximation used in practice. Minor: entropy H(tau) strictly increasing is asserted without proof; the explosive gradient lower bound is argued informally. Both are acceptable at this level.
justifications.completeness: All seven stated learning objectives are fully covered: (1) RNN vanishing-gradient derivation and LSTM resolution in §3.1–3.2; (2) scaled dot-product attention formula with sqrt(d_k) proof and multi-head attention in §4.1–4.2; (3) temperature, top-p, beam search, and financial strategy selection table in §5; (4) constrained decoding and JSON extraction with working Python listing in §6; (5) RAG pipeline with code in §8; (6) knowledge distillation, LoRA, and quantisation in §9; (7) five or more hallucination-mitigation techniques in §10. Exercise set now includes 6 exercises: one existing illustration [I], two new [B] exercises (attention numerical, sampling strategy), two new [I] exercises (LoRA budget, SBERT pipeline), and one new [A] exercise (RAG hallucination audit). All required difficulty levels present. Exercise count is lean for a 9-section chapter but meets project minimum.
justifications.pedagogy: Learning objectives are stated upfront and map cleanly to labelled sections and exercises. The deepdive/context/remark/example/definition/proposition/proof environment structure is used correctly and consistently: deepdive for mathematical derivations, context for practitioner-oriented material. Financial examples are integrated throughout (merger valuation from earnings call, covenant extraction, FinBERT benchmarks, BloombergGPT). Chapter summary and roadmap table effectively consolidate the material. New exercises cover key sub-topics mapped directly to LOs, with increasing difficulty across [B]/[I]/[A] tags. The EBNF grammar listing and RAG code listing are well-integrated pedagogical demonstrations. The SBERT and LoRA exercises reinforce core mathematical definitions with applied numerical tasks.
justifications.style: All five tables are proper table floats with \caption and \label. Numbered subsections used consistently throughout. \citet/\citep used correctly. Code listings use minted with proper captions; the EBNF grammar listing now carries \label{lst:ebnf-grammar}. Bib key liu2019roberta is correct. No \paragraph{} or inline \textbf{} paragraph-label violations. Figure fig:ch02-illustration is properly referenced in the architecture remark. Minor: several listings use the [H] float specifier which forces placement and may interact poorly with page breaks in long compiled output, but this is not a content-quality issue. Two cross-referenced API listings (lst:openai, lst:anthropic, lst:finbert) are clean. Overall style is professional and consistent.
